title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Compiler Case: Behavior Type Tests
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts behavior type tests increment in a behavior-and-type regression suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Select DOM, worker, iterable, and server type libraries that match the real runtime without leaking incompatible globals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Separate compile-time claims from runtime facts, narrow every external unknown with executable evidence, inspect diagnostics and emitted JavaScript, then test a changed schema without assertions or suppression.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"3" that must not enter the trusted model. A defensible solution applies Behavior Type Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a behavior-and-type regression suite.
